title: Boston city councilor criticizes ‘soft on crime’ progressives after Cambridge shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston City Councilor Ed Flynn criticizes progressives as 'soft on crime' following Cambridge shooting.
Tyler Brown, accused in the shooting, pleaded not guilty to several assault and firearms charges.
Flynn argues the justice system's leniency contributes to neighborhood destabilization and demands stricter accountability.

title: ‘Quality of life is plummeting’: Readers share their economic concerns
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A MassINC poll reveals 42% of Massachusetts residents feel financially worse off than a year ago.
Boston.com readers cite housing costs, healthcare, and taxes as major economic concerns impacting quality of life.
SmartAsset study shows Massachusetts has the highest income needed to remain middle class in the U.S.

title: A ‘lifestyle-focused’ restaurant opens Friday at this new Cape Cod hotel
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Gypsy Sol, a mystic-inspired restaurant, opens May 15 at The Uncommoner Hotel in West Yarmouth, Cape Cod.
The restaurant features tarot-inspired cocktails, Mediterranean dishes, and wellness events like yoga and sound baths.
Chef Mark Cina leads the menu with New England ingredients blended with global influences, offering seasonal seafood and vegetable dishes.

title: 18-year-old arrested in connection to Mother’s Day double shooting in Dorchester
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jaynell Sumler, 18, of Dorchester, arrested for Mother's Day shooting on Kingsdale St., charged with assault to murder.
The shooting hospitalized a woman with life-threatening injuries and a man with non-life-threatening injuries.
Sumler faces multiple charges including carrying a firearm without license; he pleaded not guilty on Thursday.

title: ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’ was renewed for Season 2
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' will return for a second season, Bravo confirmed on May 11.
Season 1, which premiered on April 2, became Bravo's second-biggest show behind 'The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills.'
'RHORI' received two 2026 Critics Choice Real TV Awards nominations for Best Unstructured Series and Best Ensemble Cast.

title: Alleged Memorial Drive shooter pleads not guilty from hospital bed
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Brown, 46, pleads not guilty to assault and firearms charges from his hospital bed after Memorial Drive shooting.
Two men suffered life-threatening injuries in the alleged shooting; one has been released, the other remains hospitalized.
Brown, previously on probation, has a history of violent offenses and was recently released from a psychiatric facility.

title: MassINC poll: Healey’s approval down as excitement over T improvements wanes
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey's approval rating declined, with 39% approval and 45% disapproval according to MassINC's latest poll.
82% of Massachusetts voters rated the state's transit system as fair or poor, with limited progress seen in MBTA improvements.
MassINC poll shows majority support for microtransit funding and free public transit on Election Day to boost accessibility.

title: Amelia’s Taqueria in Allston has permanently closed
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Amelia’s Taqueria in Allston closed on May 1 after operating for 14 years at 180 Brighton Ave.
The closure was announced with a note thanking customers for their support, shared by Daryl Donatelli on social media.
Amelia's Taqueria still has locations in Greater Boston on Beacon Street, Boylston Street, and Assembly Row.

title: Teen arrested after allegedly leaving JP school with loaded firearm
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 16-year-old was arrested for carrying a loaded firearm after leaving Community Academy in Jamaica Plain.
Police identified the firearm as a loaded Smith & Wesson SD9 and a high-capacity magazine was found.
The teen faces delinquency charges including possession of a firearm without a license and carrying a loaded weapon.

title: Red Sox ace Garrett Crochet provided an injury update after bullpen session
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Garrett Crochet, Red Sox pitcher, is recovering from shoulder inflammation and completed a 20-pitch bullpen session.
Crochet anticipates another bullpen session before facing live batters but has no set date for return to rotation.
He attributes the injury to bad mechanics and aims to improve them while gradually regaining shoulder health.
